skill.name: remotion-best-practices
skill.description: Best practices for Remotion - Video creation in React
skill.summary: Domain-specific knowledge for creating programmatic videos using Remotion with React
skill.author: remotion-dev

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 555 patterns that are all false positives. The backtick patterns are markdown code fences in documentation files, not shell execution. The crypto warnings hit YAML frontmatter fields. Network findings are documentation URLs and example code. This is a documentation-only skill with no executable code that poses security risks.

content.user_title: Create Programmatic Videos with Remotion and React
content.value_statement: Building videos programmatically requires understanding Remotion's composition system, animation patterns, and media handling. This skill provides domain-specific best practices for creating professional videos using React components and Remotion APIs.
actual_capabilities: Guide users on Remotion composition patterns including Sequence, AbsoluteFill, and Still components
Explain animation techniques using useCurrentFrame(), spring(), and interpolation
Provide best practices for importing and handling media assets like videos, audio, images, and fonts
Demonstrate advanced patterns for 3D content, charts, captions, and transitions
Show how to calculate metadata and dynamically set composition properties
limitations: Does not execute Remotion code or render videos directly
Cannot access external Remotion documentation beyond provided knowledge
Does not validate video file formats or codec compatibility
Cannot troubleshoot rendering issues that require system-specific debugging

use_cases.title: Social Media Video Creation
use_cases.description: Create automated video content for social platforms with consistent branding, captions, and animations using Remotion compositions
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and social media managers

use_cases.title: Data Visualization Videos
use_cases.description: Generate explainer videos with animated charts, graphs, and data visualizations that update dynamically from JSON data sources
use_cases.target_user: Data analysts and business intelligence teams

use_cases.title: Educational Content Production
use_cases.description: Build tutorial videos with synchronized captions, code highlights, and step-by-step animations for online courses

prompt_templates.title: Basic Composition Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a Remotion composition that displays a title card with animated text. I need the composition to be 1920x1080 at 30fps for 5 seconds.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setting up their first Remotion project

prompt_templates.title: Animation with Easing
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to animate an element fading in and sliding up using spring animation in Remotion. Include the useCurrentFrame and spring configuration.

prompt_templates.title: Media Asset Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to import a video file and overlay text captions on it. Show me the proper way to use the Video component and sync captions with the timeline.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating videos with multiple media layers

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Sequencing
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain how to sequence multiple scenes with transitions between them. Include trim, offset, and duration control for complex video compositions.

output_examples.input: Create a fade-in animation for text
output_examples.output: Use the spring helper with useCurrentFrame to animate opacity from 0 to 1 over the first 60 frames. Apply the interpolated value to the style.opacity property of your text element.

Import the Audio component from Remotion, use the src prop to specify your audio file path, and wrap it in your composition. Use the volume prop to control loudness and PlaybackRate for speed adjustments.

best_practices: Always drive animations with useCurrentFrame() to ensure deterministic rendering during video export
Use Sequence components with proper layout props when nesting elements to avoid rendering artifacts
Preload fonts and assets before rendering to prevent flickering or missing content in output videos
anti_patterns: Do not use useFrame() from React Three Fiber inside Remotion compositions as it causes non-deterministic rendering
Avoid inline object styles for complex animations as they cause unnecessary re-renders
Never assume browser APIs are available as Remotion renders in a headless environment

faq.question: What video formats does Remotion support?
faq.answer: Remotion supports formats that your browser can decode, typically MP4 (H.264), WebM, and MOV. Use the can-decode utility to verify format compatibility before rendering.

faq.question: How do I optimize Remotion rendering performance?
faq.answer: Use memo() for components, avoid inline functions in JSX, preload assets, and use layout='none' on Sequence components when appropriate to reduce layout calculations.

faq.question: Can I use Remotion with TypeScript?
faq.answer: Yes, Remotion has excellent TypeScript support with built-in type definitions. All hooks and components are fully typed for better developer experience.

faq.question: How do I handle different video aspect ratios?
faq.answer: Use useVideoConfig() to access composition dimensions, and calculate responsive values based on width and height. Consider using AbsoluteFill for full-coverage layouts.

faq.question: What is the difference between Sequence and AbsoluteFill?
faq.answer: Sequence controls timing and layering on the timeline, while AbsoluteFill positions elements to fill the entire composition. They serve different purposes and are often used together.

faq.question: How do I add subtitles or captions to my video?
faq.answer: Use the @remotion/captions package with importSrt() to parse SRT files, then map caption data to rendered text elements synchronized with useCurrentFrame().
